items.title: A near-autonomous AI chemist improves a challenging reaction in medicinal chemistry
items.lead_paragraph: OpenAI and Molecule.one show how a near-autonomous AI chemist using GPT-5.4 improved a key drug-making reaction, advancing medicinal chemistry research.

items.title: Introducing LifeSciBench
items.lead_paragraph: Introducing LifeSciBench, an expert-authored, expert-reviewed benchmark for evaluating how AI systems handle real-world life science research tasks and decisions.

items.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
items.lead_paragraph: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.

items.title: Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
items.lead_paragraph: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.

items.title: New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
items.lead_paragraph: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.

items.title: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
items.lead_paragraph: Preply uses OpenAI to launch AI-generated lesson summaries, providing personalised feedback and language learning exercises.

items.title: OpenAI to acquire Ona
items.lead_paragraph: OpenAI plans to acquire Ona to expand Codex with secure, persistent cloud environments, enabling long-running AI agents across enterprise workflows.

items.title: How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes
items.lead_paragraph: Discover how astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan uses Codex to build black hole simulations, helping scientists study extreme physics and test Einstein’s theory of general relativity.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.lead_paragraph: OpenAI supports the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency, advancing provenance standards and tools to help people understand AI-generated content.

items.title: BBVA puts AI at the core of banking with OpenAI
items.lead_paragraph: Learn how BBVA scaled ChatGPT Enterprise to 100,000 employees and partnered with OpenAI to accelerate AI-powered banking transformation worldwide.

items.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
items.lead_paragraph: UK government partners with Google DeepMind to build a new AI-powered prototype aimed at faster housing decisions.

items.title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
From the Hugging Face Hub to robot hardware with Strands Agents and LeRobot
GLM-5.2: Built for Long-Horizon Tasks
Agentic Resource Discovery: Let agents search
Profiling in PyTorch (Part 2): From nn.Linear to a Fused MLP
Anthropic opens Seoul office and announces new partnerships across the Korean AI ecosystem
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: Anthropic opens Seoul and announces new partnerships across the Korean AI ecosystem—with the enterprises, startups, and researchers behind some of the most ambitious deployments of Claude.

items.title: Developing nuclear safeguards for AI through public-private partnership
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: Together with the NNSA and DOE national laboratories, we have co-developed a classifier—an AI system that automatically categorizes content—that distinguishes between concerning and benign nuclear-related conversations with 96% accuracy in preliminary testing.

items.title: TCS and Anthropic partner to bring Claude to regulated industries
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: We’re announcing a partnership with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS). TCS will provide Claude to 50,000 of its own employees across 56 countries; build Claude-powered products for clients in financial services, healthcare, the public sector, and other regulated industries; and join the Claude Partner

items.title: Core views on AI safety: When, why, what, and how
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: AI progress may lead to transformative AI systems in the next decade, but we do not yet understand how to make such systems safe and aligned with human values. In response, we are pursuing a variety of research directions aimed at better understanding, evaluating, and aligning AI systems.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5: a Mythos-class model that we’ve made safe for general use.

items.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: The US government has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States.

items.title: Results from first Anthropic Public Record
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: Anthropic Public Record is a national survey of attitudes and opinions towards AI.

items.title: DXC will integrate Claude into the systems banks, airlines, and other regulated industries rely on
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: We’re announcing a multi-year global alliance with DXC Technology, one of the world’s largest IT services companies.

items.title: Introducing Claude Corps
items.author: Anthropic
items.lead_paragraph: We’re launching Claude Corps, a national fellowship program for people early in their careers who are passionate about extending the benefits of AI to communities across America.
